mechanism_narrative: Robust and sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS shows no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.05) with SPX 20-day returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions often follow sharp UNRATE increases
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates an overly accommodative monetary policy that can lead to asset bubbles or future aggressive tightening, creating market uncertainty and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) when CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: In a period of economic expansion, sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity and corporate capacity utilization, signaling healthy earnings prospects and driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month growth above 0.2% in an expansionary regime does not lead to positive SPX 5-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) signifies overall economic expansion and increasing aggregate demand, which underpins corporate profitability and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 year-over-year growth shows no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with SPX 60-day returns over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a significant weakening of the labor market, signaling a potential recession and reduced consumer purchasing power, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:significant job losses often precede economic contractions
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of months where PAYEMS declines by more than 50,000 persons.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, an increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signals aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, which raises borrowing costs, dampens economic activity, and leads to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in a month AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 2.5%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with high inflation
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.2%) following a FEDFUNDS hike of >0.25% when CPIAUCSL annual growth is above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad-based contraction in both investment and consumer demand, signaling a severe economic slowdown that is highly detrimental to corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average change < 0 AND RSAFS 3-month average growth < 0
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 35% of periods where both HOUST and RSAFS show negative 3-month average trends.
